short_summary: NATO cuts persist, Iran deal closer
rationale: US NATO asset reductions remain the dominant concern for Romania's Black Sea flank; Iran deal progress is a mild positive for energy costs, no new escalation in the region.

top_events.title: US to cut air and naval assets for NATO Europe
top_events.summary: No new details this cycle, but the confirmed US reduction in NATO Europe assets directly affects Romania's Black Sea security posture and regional deterrence against Russia.

top_events.title: US-Iran deal reported closer than ever
top_events.summary: A US-Iran deal could lower global energy prices, providing indirect relief to Romanian households facing elevated energy costs.

top_events.title: Pro-Russian opposition party calls for Armenian election result annulment
top_events.summary: Political instability in Armenia is a minor regional signal; limited direct impact on Romania but worth monitoring as part of broader post-Soviet instability near the Black Sea region.
